locations.name: 查塔姆群岛
locations.summary: 查塔姆群岛（莫里奥里语：Rekohu；毛利語：Wharekauri；英語：Chatham Islands）是新西兰的一个群岛，由40千米范围内的约10个大小岛屿组成。这些位于新西兰东南方800多公里远的岛屿，于1982年正式归属新西兰管辖…

locations.name: 新西兰
locations.summary: 新西兰（英語：New Zealand）又称奥特亚罗瓦（毛利語：Aotearoa，直译：「长白云之乡」），位於太平洋西南部的主權島國，是英联邦新西蘭王國的主體部分，首都為惠灵顿，最大的城市為奧克蘭。紐西蘭主要由兩大島嶼組成：北島（Te Ik…

locations.name: Little Mangere Island
locations.summary: Little Mangere is a small island of the Chatham Archipelago, just off the western end of Mangere Island, about 4 km (2½…

locations.name: The Sisters (New Zealand)
locations.summary: The Sisters is a group of three main islands located 16 kilometres (10 mi) north of Cape Pattison, Chatham Island.

locations.name: Rabbit Island (Chatham Islands)
locations.summary: Rabbit Island is a rocky islet lying off Tarawhenua Point on the north-west coast of Pitt Island in the Chatham Islands…

locations.name: Te Whanga Lagoon
locations.summary: Te Whanga Lagoon dominates the geography of Chatham Island, in the South Pacific Ocean off New Zealand's east coast.

relations.subject: Pitt shag
relations.evidence: The Pitt shag is native to the Chatham Islands in New Zealand, islands such as the Chatham, Pitt, Rangatira, Mangere, Little Mangere, Western Reef, Pyramid, Sisters, Murumuru, Castle, Rabbit, Forty Fours and Star Keys. On an island the bird is located along the coast, on rocky islets and over nearby waters where breeding and foraging takes place such as the Te Whanga Lagoon

relations.subject: Pitt shag
relations.evidence: The Pitt shag is threatened by habitat loss due to predation and climate change causing a decline from 1997 to 2012, but there is a recovery plan by the Department of Conservation
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Pitt shag

relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

relations.subject: Pitt shag
relations.evidence: Threat The Pitt shag is claimed endangered due to its loss of breeding habitat and rapidly declining, small population
relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。
